title: Two-phase configuration migration and restart recovery

phase1.prompt: This is phase 1 of a controlled two-session coding evaluation. Read SPEC.md and the existing code. Use update_plan append_step operations with one stable goal_id and the current expected_revision to create a durable plan with at least four concrete steps covering both sessions. Give every step a stable id, explicit dependencies, and acceptance criteria. In this phase, implement and verify parseLegacyConfig, applyOverrides, and serializeConfig in src/config.js. Run npm test. Do not modify SPEC.md or any file under test/. Before completing this scope, mark every CLI/session-2 step deferred with a concrete status_reason. In the final update_step, mark the current phase complete, attach the successful npm test to that step's evidence, and set current_scope_complete to true only after every step is resolved. After that tool result returns, provide a concise final answer with no further tool calls.

phase2.prompt: Continue the same configuration migration task after the server restart. Recover the current goal_id, plan revision, steps, messages, workspace, and git diff. Reopen the deferred CLI/session-2 steps before starting them. Implement the remaining CLI contract in src/cli.js, repair incomplete library behavior, and run npm test. Do not modify SPEC.md or any file under test/. After npm test passes, use update_plan update_step operations with the current expected_revision to mark every remaining step completed and attach concrete evidence to each completed step. Set current_scope_complete to true on the final update. After that tool result returns, provide a concise final answer with no further tool calls. Do not claim completion unless tests pass.
